title: v17-T3 L3 Coupling metric enforcement (.rust-audit.toml + CI gate)
author: KooshaPari
device_rationale: Pure config (TOML) + bash + GitHub Actions YAML + Markdown; no cargo build, no cargo test, no heavy simulation
device_gate_self_check: Rule 1 PASS / Rule 2 N/A / Rule 3 PASS
pillar_description: Coupling — efferent coupling (Ca) per module, leading static indicator of substrate drift
convention_matches: scripts/check-coupling.sh follows the scripts/cache_stats_wrapper.sh + scripts/validate-ssot.sh bash pattern (set -euo pipefail; finding-friendly output)
.github/workflows/coupling.yml matches the .github/workflows/deny.yml + chaos-gate.yml pattern (dtolnay/rust-toolchain@stable, cancel-in-progress concurrency, findindings-upload as artifact)
docs/architecture/coupling-metric.md slots next to docs/architecture/CLEAN_LAYER.md, EXTENSION_LAYER.md, PRINCIPLES.md
out_of_scope: rust-toolchain.toml — task referenced it but it does not exist at the monorepo root; toolchain pinned via dtolnay/rust-toolchain@stable (matches existing deny.yml pattern) and documented in the workflow comment
touching parallel-agent branches (chore/L25-loom-tests-*, chore/L5-114-*, chore/T2-v14-*, chore/T4-v14-*)
running cargo build or cargo test (out of scope per task instructions)
any modification outside the 4 new files + this worklog

test_results.shellcheck_sanity: SKIPPED — shellcheck not required for landing; pattern matches existing validate-ssot.sh and cache_stats_wrapper.sh
test_results.yaml_parse: PASS — coupling.yml parses as valid GitHub Actions workflow
test_results.toml_parse: PASS — .rust-audit.toml parses with stdlib tomllib (Python 3.11+) — verified by reading structure into the shell parser

follow_ups.description: When rust-toolchain.toml is added at monorepo root, switch coupling.yml from dtolnay/rust-toolchain@stable to per-file pinning
Wire coupling.yml as a required check in branch protection settings once v17 wave A lands
Add a just check-coupling target (mirrors just validate-ssot / just cache-stats / just bench pattern from v12)

summary: L3 (Coupling) pillar fully enforced at the substrate tier. .rust-audit.toml codifies max_coupling=5 / max_cyclo=15 / max_fn_lines=80 / fail_on_warning=true. scripts/check-coupling.sh wraps cargo-modules structure with --dry-run + --crate + --help flags. .github/workflows/coupling.yml triggers on PR touching any pheno-* Cargo.toml or src/lib.rs, runs the script, and uploads findings/coupling-report.json as a 30-day artifact. docs/architecture/coupling-metric.md explains the metric, the thresholds, and the triage playbook. Zero modifications to any pre-existing file.
